commit_scope: Let native Form handlers return first-class KernelHTTPResponse cells so the Rust kernel router emits exact status, headers, content type, and body instead of coercing every native response to 200.

commands: cd form && native prepared Go/Rust parity for kernel-http-band
cd form/form-kernel-rust && temporary form.route manifest returning kh-response(418, headers, body) served /teapot
cd form/form-kernel-rust && production manifest served /api/utils/grounded_cost mismatch as 422 via kh-response
local_validation.notes: Rust cargo test passed with 41 tests, including KernelHTTPResponse parser coverage and compatibility for the older status tag. Native prepared Go/Rust parity for kernel-http-band passed with go=66690 rust=66690. Temporary manifest smoke returned HTTP 418, Content-Type application/problem+json, X-Kernel-Response native, body {"detail":"teapot"}, and X-Form-Router native-kernel. Production manifest smoke returned HTTP 422, Content-Type application/json, X-Form-Router native-kernel, and the grounded_cost mismatch body.

ci_validation.notes: Remote CI is pending until the branch is pushed and a PR is opened.

deploy_validation.notes: Production verification is pending until PR checks pass, the branch is merged, deployment completes, and public verification runs.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Native kernel-router handlers can return KernelHTTPResponse(status, headers, body) as a Form value, and the HTTP front door emits the exact status line, content type, filtered end-to-end headers, router provenance header, content length, and body.
test_flows: native prepared Go/Rust parity for kernel-http-band
temporary form.route local router smoke returning kh-response(418, headers, body)
production manifest local router smoke returning 422 from /api/utils/grounded_cost mismatch

phase_gate.notes: Local native proof and local HTTP proof are complete; CI, merge, deploy, and public verification remain pending.

evidence_refs: Rust serve now parses kh-response tagged KernelHTTPResponse values before falling back to the older respond status tag.

Native response headers carry Content-Type through the dedicated response content-type slot and relay filtered end-to-end headers while the router keeps Content-Length, Connection, and X-Form-Router under host control.
The production respond helper now returns kh-response with application/json, so existing promoted 400/422 routes exercise the first-class response path.
The temporary form.route smoke proved a non-production route can emit 418 plus a custom header directly from a Form handler.
The production grounded_cost smoke proved a real promoted route now emits its 422 JSON error through KernelHTTPResponse.
